_human_review.notes: Project owner instructed Codex to mark the SA Stage B generated/reconciled tests as approved for internal/project benchmarking after manual review on 2026-06-05. This is not legal advice, external counsel review, or procurement-grade legal approval; candidate labels remain separate from locked recall baselines.

_label_note: AUTO-LABELED by LLM. Spot-check before refreshing recall.lock.json. Lock updates must include this provenance in --reason per item 16.
_label_tier_note: SA Stage B cleanup: must_detect is the strict detector-aligned benchmark tier; ideal_must_detect preserves broader auto-labeled statutory coverage for gap discovery. ideal_must_detect is not a locked recall baseline. Stage B labels are project-owner approved for internal benchmarking.

_label_warning_resolution.reason: SA Stage B strict tier was rebuilt from deterministic strict-mode engine findings; raw Azure auto-label warnings no longer describe active must_detect labels. Broader statutory coverage remains in ideal_must_detect for gap review.

_strict_reconciliation.basis: SA Stage B auto-label strict tier was reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine findings; original auto-label breadth is preserved in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. This is internal benchmark cleanup, not legal advice or procurement-grade review.

ideal_must_detect.reason: moved from must_detect after strict runtime no longer emitted exact span during Stage B baseline reconciliation

must_detect.matched_text: The mailbox also contained non-public issuer materials, including draft Q2 revenue guidance and a board memo on a contemplated green sukuk programme, not yet announced on the Saudi Exchange (Tadawul).

Pursuant to PDPL and SDAIA guidance, we lodged an initial breach notification within 72 hours and evaluated cross-border data transfer; SDAIA transfer request TR-1447-0021 for forensic triage in Abu Dhabi was prepared but not executed.

Mr. Nabil Al-Qatiri (Head of Compliance) detected anomalous SMTP forwarding rules and escalated per the Tadawul material-event workflow.
Data at risk included: (a) one HR onboarding file with Iqama No. 2439184756 for Mr. Tariq Al-Bishri (technician), (b) one vendor settlement sheet listing IBAN SA18 7000 0000 1234 5678 9012 for Desert Grid Services Co. (LLC), and (c) a passport scan for Ms. Hana Kareem (Passport No. XZ1234567).
The mailbox also contained non-public issuer materials, including draft Q2 revenue guidance and a board memo on a contemplated green sukuk programme, not yet announced on the Saudi Exchange (Tadawul).
Under PDPL and CMA rules, Zawaya treated this as a material cyber incident with potential MNPI exposure and initiated issuer disclosure governance via the Board Secretary.
The CMA Market Conduct Department was notified through the Issuer e-Portal at 22:15 AST under Case ID MC-26-00054; trading restrictions or blackout decisions remain internal and are not disclosed here.
Pursuant to PDPL and SDAIA guidance, we lodged an initial breach notification within 72 hours and evaluated cross-border data transfer; SDAIA transfer request TR-1447-0021 for forensic triage in Abu Dhabi was prepared but not executed.
No evidence indicates exfiltration outside KSA; logs show two message previews and zero attachments downloaded; our SOC retains copies in Riyadh only.
The phrase “National ID: ________” appears on legacy onboarding templates and is not tied to any individual record referenced in this report.
One development dataset contained “Iqama 2000000000” (invalid checksum, placeholder in a staging file) and a publicly posted customer helpline 800-111-2222; neither constitute live personal data.
For data-subject inquiries under PDPL, contact Dr. Lamis Al-Dahiri (DPO) at dpo@zawaya-ws.sa or +966 55 000 9876; the CFO may be reached via switchboard at +966 11 000 0000.
Access credentials were reset, forwarding rules purged, and internal acknowledgement letters issued to affected data subjects and the vendor.
No financial loss occurred; however, we are enhancing issuer workflow controls, adding SDAIA-approved data transfer assessments, and updating the insider list maintained for the sukuk workstream.
